L1.area: Extension wrappers and native database API integration

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where a temporary C-compatible string object is converted to a raw pointer and immediately deallocated, leaving a dangling pointer that is subsequently passed to a native API function call. This can lead to memory corruption, potentially allowing an attacker to achieve arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: Database session extension bindings and change-set management
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the database session extension bindings where temporary C-compatible string objects are converted to raw pointers in the same expression, causing the temporary to be deallocated while the dangling pointer is subsequently passed to native session management API calls. This can lead to memory corruption, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: In the session extension bindings, temporary CString objects created by str_to_cstring() are immediately converted to raw pointers via .as_ptr() in the same expression, causing the CString to be dropped while the pointer is still used in subsequent FFI calls.
